summary: Header-only cleanup that removed duplicate or redundant declarations across 31 files, mostly in fighter, item, lb, stage, Dolphin mtx, and sysdolphin/baselib headers. The PR was motivated by clang-tidy's readability-redundant-declaration check. It preserved the better-positioned or better-documented declaration in several cases, added missing forward-header inclusion for scene declarations, and left a note that more redundant declarations remained, especially in melee/ft/ft_08A4.h.
what_changed: Removed 112 lines and added 3 lines across 31 changed headers; almost all changes deleted duplicate function prototypes, duplicate extern declarations, or stale forward declarations.

In src/dolphin/mtx.h, removed duplicate declarations for PSMTXTrans, PSMTXScale, and PSMTXQuat while keeping the later versions with more descriptive parameter names such as x_trans, y_trans, z_trans, x_scale, y_scale, and z_scale.

In src/melee/ft/ftcommon.h, removed a block of repeated prototypes already declared elsewhere in the same header, while retaining documented declarations such as ftCommon_8007FE84 and ftCommon_800804EC near their comments. Added a single ftCommon_8007D5D4 declaration near related ftCommon_8007D5xx functions after removing its duplicate from the later repeated block.
In src/melee/ft/ftcamera.h, collapsed duplicate ftCamera_80076064 declarations into a single prototype, choosing the unnamed-parameter form shown in the diff.
In src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h, removed an exact duplicate ftCo_800D6C60 prototype and kept the version with the named callback parameter.
In item and library headers such as src/melee/it/it_266F.h, src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.h, and src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h, removed repeated declarations for functions or extern callback symbols.

In sysdolphin/baselib headers including class.h, fog.h, gobj.h, gobjproc.h, shadow.h, sislib.h, texp.h, jobj.h, debug.h, and lobj.h, removed duplicate baselib prototypes while preserving one declaration for each symbol.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h, removed forward typedefs for DynamicModelDesc, SceneDesc, and StaticModelDesc from the generic baselib forward header.
In src/melee/sc/scene.h, added #include <melee/sc/forward.h>, apparently to source scene-specific forward declarations from the scene module after removing them from baselib/forward.h.
smart_moves: Used clang-tidy readability-redundant-declaration to find low-risk cleanup opportunities systematically instead of manually hunting duplicates.

Kept declarations where nearby comments or parameter names added useful decomp context, for example preserving ftCo_800D6C60 with the named callback parameter and ftCommon_800804EC near its @todo and @returns documentation.
Moved scene-specific forward declarations out of the broad baselib forward header path and into the scene header via <melee/sc/forward.h>, reducing cross-system leakage.
Made a narrowly scoped cleanup PR with no behavioral code changes, which is appropriate for declaration hygiene because it reduces review and matching risk.
Called out that the clang-tidy pass was incomplete and that more issues remained, especially in melee/ft/ft_08A4.h, giving future cleanup work a clear target.
decomp_lessons: Duplicate prototypes create noise in decomp headers and can obscure which declaration has the best parameter names or documentation; prefer a single declaration located near related APIs or comments.
When removing redundant declarations, preserve the declaration with the most useful recovered information, such as named parameters, comments, or better grouping.
Do not use broad project-wide forward headers as dumping grounds for module-specific types; move module-specific forward declarations to the owning subsystem, as done with scene forward declarations.
Static analysis checks like clang-tidy readability-redundant-declaration are useful for decomp maintenance even when they do not directly improve matching.
Header cleanups should be kept behavior-neutral: remove repeated declarations without changing function signatures unless there is direct evidence that one signature is clearly canonical.
If a cleanup reveals remaining redundant declarations but not all are safe or convenient to remove, document the remaining hotspot for follow-up rather than over-expanding the PR.
naming_conventions: Existing decomp symbol naming was preserved: unresolved functions kept address-based names such as ftCommon_8007FE84, it_802728C8, HSD_GObj_80390ED0, and HSD_SisLib_803A67EC.
Subsystem prefixes remained consistent: ftCommon_, ftCamera_, ftColl_, Fighter_, Item_, grLib_, grDynamicAttr_, lbAudioAx_, lbRefract_, HSD_, PSMTX, and PSVEC.

For duplicate declarations, parameter naming can guide which declaration to keep: examples include ftCo_800D6C60(ftCo_GObj*, HSD_GObjEvent callback) and matrix transforms with x_trans/y_trans/z_trans or x_scale/y_scale/z_scale.
Forward declarations should live under the owning module where possible, indicated by adding <melee/sc/forward.h> instead of keeping SceneDesc/StaticModelDesc in <baselib/forward.h>.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were discussed in the PR context; this was a header declaration cleanup with no apparent codegen changes.
The main tactic was static-analysis-assisted cleanup using clang-tidy readability-redundant-declaration, which can be reused to reduce header noise before or during decomp work.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining clang-tidy readability-redundant-declaration warnings, especially in src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h as noted by the PR body.
Audit broad forward headers for subsystem-specific typedefs that should move to owning module forward headers.
When touching headers, check whether duplicate prototypes differ only by parameter names and keep the most informative declaration.
Search for repeated extern callback declarations similar to lbRefract_80021FB4.
